framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen → /sector → /comps → /catalysts → /thesis → portfolio). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).

regime.label: Hawkish Fed, momentum melt-up in semis
regime.note: The Fed held but signaled a possible 2026 hike (~half the FOMC) as the Middle-East inflation impulse lingers, even with the US–Iran ceasefire pulling oil back. Higher-for-longer pressures the richest long-duration names hardest — which argues against chasing 400–500× semis and toward reasonable-multiple quality plus a real cash buffer.

thesis: The screen is dominated by semis at fresh 52-week highs — ARM (+88% over 20d) trades at a 495× P/E, MRVL at 99×, AMD at 168×. The financial-services lens declines to chase those: at a possible-hike turn, that's where the air is thinnest. Instead the book anchors on NVDA, the valuation anomaly of the group — 31× P/E, ~26% below its high despite +85% YoY data-center growth — i.e. the quality name on sale while the crowd pays up elsewhere. It adds MU (49× P/E) as the cheapest expression of the memory super-cycle, and GE Aerospace (44×, dividend) as a non-tech momentum sleeve that also hedges the hawkish/geopolitical regime. ARM and MRNA (no earnings) are deliberately screened out on valuation/quality. ~48% cash reflects a stretched tape into a hawkish Fed.

evidence.point: ARM 495× P/E at a fresh 52-wk high — screened out on valuation
NVDA 31× P/E, ~26% below 52-wk high — cheapest quality in the group
MU 49× P/E, +49% 20d — cheapest expression of the memory super-cycle
GE Aerospace 44× P/E + dividend — non-tech momentum & macro hedge
Fed signaled a possible 2026 hike — headwind for the richest multiples

risks: Momentum keeps ripping: by refusing ARM/MRVL at the highs, this book underperforms if the melt-up continues.
MU is memory-cyclical — pricing rolls over fast when the cycle turns.
A ~48% cash weight drags badly if the ceasefire fuels a broad melt-up.

data_examined.label: Screen: 20d/5d momentum + vs-20d-SMA across the 100-name universe
Comps: P/E, P/B, market cap, 52-wk range on the finalists
Regime: Fed path, oil/gold reaction to the Iran ceasefire

generated_by: Claude for Financial Services (equity-research plugin methodology) via Claude Code, 2026-06-18
note: Daily bars run through the 2026-06-17 close; fundamentals are live 2026-06-18. The competitor books advance on the next completed session — this report sets the analyst's next-tick targets.
